user_message: The CSA escalated Nod Publishers to me -- can you map the stakeholders?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-10 13:13 EST
Subject: Re: Re: renewal risk Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with renewal risk. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 16:04 EST
Subject: Follow-up: renewal risk Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about renewal risk. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: renewal risk Discussion - Nod Publishers
Date: 2024-02-06
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Michelle (Nod Publishers)

[00:02:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on renewal risk from our last session.

[00:07:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:17:00] Michelle (Nod Publishers): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:19:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:21:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:25:00] Michelle (Nod Publishers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:28:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the renewal risk front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Nod Publishers: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Stakeholder Map Generation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
